pattern: Track the positive numbers seen so far in most-recent-first order, and each time a -1 shows up, count how many -1's have appeared back-to-back and use that count to look up how far back into the recent history to reach.
hints: Keep a running list of the positive numbers you've encountered, and separately track how many -1's in a row you're currently inside of -- that streak resets the moment a positive number appears.
The k-th `-1` in the current streak should read the k-th most recently seen positive number, not the k-th positive number overall from the start.
You don't need to physically shift a list to keep 'most recent' at the front -- appending new positives to the end and reading the k-th from the end gives the same answer with less work.

edge_inputs.description: three consecutive -1's exhaust the two seen positives, last one falls back to -1
streak of -1's interrupted by a positive number resets the streak length
single positive element, no -1's at all
single -1 with nothing seen yet, must fall back to -1
leading run of -1's before any positive number has ever appeared
every element is -1, seen stays empty the whole time
long streak of -1's that eventually exceeds how many positives have been seen
alternating positive and -1, so every streak length is exactly one
maximum-length array of alternating values at the extreme allowed value

stress.description: random mix of -1's and positive values up to the maximum length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.choice([-1, rng.randint(1, 100)]) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

stress.description: long runs of -1's interspersed with occasional positives to stress the streak reset
